Based on the newly gathered information, I find no significant reasons to change my agreement with Option C - Vibrio cholerae. Both the original reasoning about the presence of a distinct single central flagellum aligns perfectly with what we now know about Vibrio species. Additionally, several pieces of literature emphasize that Vibrio cholerae indeed exhibits unique motility characteristics due primarily to its polar flagellation pattern, making it stand out amongst the given choices regarding locomotion mechanisms. Therefore, maintaining confidence in Vibrio cholerae remains consistent across all available data points discussed today. Hence,
\[ \boxed{\text{I stick with } \textbf{C} }. \]
